name: Lemon-Pepper Barbecued Chicken
recipeIngredient: 8 Chicken breast halves or legs with thighs
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Remove skin from chicken and discard.
3. Trim off excess fat.
4. Brush each piece of chicken with about 2 tablespoons of Lemon-Pepper Barbecue Sauce.
5. Arrange the pieces in a single layer in a baking pan.
6. Bake for 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until juices run clear when pierced at the thickest part.
- Per serving (chicken breast): 171 calories, 27 grams protein, 5 grams carbohydrate, 4 grams fat, 73 milligrams cholesterol, 235 milligrams sodium.
- Per serving (chicken leg and attached thigh): 336 calories, 40 grams protein, 7 grams carbohydrate, 16 grams fat, 138 milligrams cholesterol, 476 milligrams sodium.
Lemon Pepper Barbecue Sauce
Makes 1 cup (Enough to cover 8 chicken pieces)
1. In a small heavy saucepan over medium heat, cook onion in oil until softened, stirring often.
2. Stir in garlic and cook for an additional minute.
3. Add tomato sauce, brown sugar, basil, mustard, paprika, black pepper, cayenne pepper, ground allspice, lemon zest, lemon juice, and vinegar to the pan.
4. Bring mixture to a simmer over the lowest heat setting.
5. Cook covered for 15 minutes until cooled to room temperature (use immediately) or refrigerate in a glass or plastic container for up to 2 weeks.
- Per 2-tablespoon serving: 29 calories, 1 gram protein, 5 grams carbohydrate, 1 gram fat, 0 cholesterol, 172 milligrams sodium.
